rationale: Tests whether bond market volatility provides a more accurate exit signal for rate-sensitive tech equities than traditional equity volatility.
expected_failure_mode: The MOVE index may decouple from tech equities during purely growth-driven selloffs, resulting in missed drawdowns.
stop_conditions: Win rate falls below 40% in the 2022 rate-hike regime holdout.
Sharpe ratio is lower than a standard VIX-based exit on the QQQ baseline.

rationale: Evaluates if the rate of change in high-yield corporate credit spreads can front-run systemic liquidity drains affecting leveraged tech ETFs.
expected_failure_mode: Credit markets may experience isolated shocks that trigger false exits in equities during otherwise strong tech bull runs.
stop_conditions: Generates more than 3 false positive exits per year during the 2010-2019 bull market.
Drawdown capture is less than 15% better than buy-and-hold.

rationale: Hypothesizes that three consecutive large overnight gaps down in QQQ indicate structural capitulation, providing a high-probability TECL entry.
expected_failure_mode: In severe structural bear markets, consecutive down gaps may merely signal the beginning of a larger volatility expansion.
stop_conditions: Average trade duration exceeds 10 days without achieving profitability.
Maximum drawdown during the trade window exceeds 15%.

rationale: Assesses whether isolated spikes in the equity put/call ratio during an established uptrend serve as reliable mean-reversion entry markers.
expected_failure_mode: The put/call ratio may remain elevated for extended periods during actual market tops, trapping early buyers.
stop_conditions: The strategy buys into more than 50% of the top-decile drawdowns.
Fails to beat QQQ buy-and-hold share accumulation over a 3-year rolling window.

rationale: Hypothesizes that a sudden spike in Utilities relative to Tech predicts a delayed structural tech selloff.
expected_failure_mode: Yield-seeking behavior may drive Utilities up independently of Tech fundamentals, causing premature exits.
stop_conditions: Correlation between Utilities spikes and Tech drops is below 0.3 over a 10-year span.
Generates excessive portfolio churn over 20 signals per year.

rationale: Evaluates if semiconductor equipment manufacturers systematically lead broader technology index trend reversals.
expected_failure_mode: Semiconductor cycles might decouple from broader software-heavy indices, rendering the lead-lag relationship noisy.
stop_conditions: Lead time is less than 1 trading session on average.
Signal accuracy degrades significantly post-2020.

rationale: Tests if sharp rejections at major technical resistance in the US Dollar Index reliably mark tradable bottoms in leveraged tech.
expected_failure_mode: A falling dollar may sometimes accompany a systemic growth shock, causing both DXY and Tech to fall simultaneously.
stop_conditions: Fails to capture at least 3 major tech rebounds during 2015-2024.
False positive rate exceeds 40%.

rationale: Tests if systematically accumulating shares immediately following major tech earnings implied-volatility crushes outperforms continuous holding.
expected_failure_mode: Earnings gaps may dictate new medium-term trends that IV crush ignores, leading to accumulation during persistent selloffs.
stop_conditions: Strategy performance is statistically indistinguishable from a random entry model.
Fails to outperform QQQ baseline holding during Q2 and Q3.

rationale: Evaluates if buying TECL after three sequential lower opens accurately captures structural crash rebounds.
expected_failure_mode: In severe shocks, sequential down gaps can persist far longer than the strategy can endure.
stop_conditions: Max drawdown exceeds 40% during execution windows.
Win rate on rebound trades is below 60%.

rationale: Tests if daily oversold conditions resolving upward while weekly momentum remains overbought creates optimal swing-trade entries.
expected_failure_mode: The weekly overbought condition may begin deteriorating, turning the daily oversold bounce into a lower-high bull trap.
stop_conditions: The strategy generates more than 5 consecutive losses during a market transition phase.
Fails to maintain positive expectancy over 50 or more trades.

rationale: Hypothesizes that market-maker net gamma flipping from negative to positive marks the end of a high-volatility drawdown regime.
expected_failure_mode: Gamma models based on open interest are estimates and may signal flips prematurely due to hidden OTC positioning.
stop_conditions: Signal lag exceeds 2 days from actual price bottoms.
Produces lower share accumulation than a simple 200 SMA cross.

rationale: Tests if the speed of capital moving out of Energy and Materials into Tech serves as a more reliable entry signal than price alone.
expected_failure_mode: Rapid rotation could be temporary rebalancing rather than a sustained macro regime shift, leading to whipsaws.
stop_conditions: Turnover rate exceeds 100% per month without corresponding profit.
Correlation with standard momentum strategies exceeds 0.85.

rationale: Evaluates if Nasdaq advance-decline divergence against QQQ new highs provides an early exit signal for leveraged holdings.
expected_failure_mode: Mega-cap tech can drag indices higher for years despite poor breadth, causing massive missed bull runs.
stop_conditions: Strategy remains in cash for more than 40% of a known bull market.
Misses more than 30% of total buy-and-hold upside.

rationale: Tests whether limiting TECL exposure to steep VIX-futures contango periods eliminates major drawdowns.
expected_failure_mode: Contango can persist during slow grinding bear markets, exposing the strategy to leveraged decay without triggering an exit.
stop_conditions: Drawdown exceeds 25% while the VIX curve remains in contango.
Fails to accumulate shares during the 2017 low-volatility melt-up.

rationale: Hypothesizes that changes in Fed balance sheet and Treasury General Account proxies correlate directly with leveraged tech performance.
expected_failure_mode: Liquidity data is published with lag, and markets may price balance-sheet operations before the data is available.
stop_conditions: Lagged data causes the strategy to enter trades post-rally.
Sharpe ratio is lower than buy-and-hold during Quantitative Tightening regimes.

rationale: Tests if extreme compression of multiple short-term moving averages in QQQ precedes tradable volatility expansions in TECL.
expected_failure_mode: Compression often resolves with false breakouts before reversing into the true trend direction.
stop_conditions: False breakout rate exceeds 60%.
Expected value after compression is negative over a 10-year test.

rationale: Evaluates if applying mean-reversion rules only when the Hurst exponent signals anti-persistence improves rebound capture.
expected_failure_mode: The Hurst exponent is sensitive to lookback windows and may misclassify trends as random walks.
stop_conditions: Parameter sensitivity shows high variance across lookback windows.
Strategy fails to out-accumulate simple RSI mean reversion.

rationale: Tests whether a drop in fractal dimension reliably identifies the onset of a sustained trend for leverage entry.
expected_failure_mode: Fractal dimension can be unstable on daily data, producing noisy non-actionable state changes.
stop_conditions: Signal flips more than 3 times a week on average.
Cumulative share accumulation falls behind the moving-average benchmark.

rationale: Tests if buying TECL in the last three days of a month following a large bond rally captures equity rebalancing flows.
expected_failure_mode: End-of-month rebalancing flows can be dwarfed by macro news or unexpected economic data.
stop_conditions: Average return of the 3-day window is lower than a random 3-day window.
Strategy has negative expectancy in the last 5 years.

rationale: Evaluates if 90%+ down-volume days followed by a higher open mark definitive tradable crash bottoms.
expected_failure_mode: Bear markets often feature massive down-volume days followed by dead-cat bounces.
stop_conditions: Holding periods generated by the signal produce drawdowns exceeding 20%.
Signal frequency is less than 1 per year, making it statistically weak.

rationale: Tests whether realized volatility breaking outside a historical probability cone signals the start of a momentum super-trend.
expected_failure_mode: Volatility breaking the cone could be a short-lived shock that reverts immediately, punishing leveraged entries.
stop_conditions: The strategy buys the top of more than 10 major blow-off peaks.
Fails to accumulate shares during the slow low-volatility uptrend of 2017.

rationale: Hypothesizes that tech exposure should be reduced when the Gold/Copper ratio spikes, signaling deflationary growth fears.
expected_failure_mode: The Gold/Copper ratio may be driven by commodity-specific supply shocks rather than macro deflation fears.
stop_conditions: Correlation between ratio spikes and QQQ drawdowns falls below 0.4.
TECL share count is negatively impacted during inflationary tech rallies.

rationale: Tests if entering TECL on the breakout of three consecutive inside days captures high-probability volatility expansion.
expected_failure_mode: Inside days can resolve into a false breakout before violently reversing.
stop_conditions: False breakout trap rate is above 45%.
Total trades over a 10-year period are too few to pass statistical significance.

rationale: Tests whether extreme CBOE SKEW readings indicate over-hedged institutional fear and provide a contrarian long entry.
expected_failure_mode: SKEW can remain structurally elevated for years due to changing hedging behavior.
stop_conditions: The strategy fails to exit cash for more than 12 consecutive months.
Share accumulation trails buy-and-hold during the 2020-2021 bull run.

rationale: Hypothesizes that sharp spikes in index implied correlation mark indiscriminate panic selling and rebound accumulation zones.
expected_failure_mode: Implied correlation can rise during a coordinated bear-market transition, turning the buy signal into a value trap.
stop_conditions: Fails to capture the COVID-19 or 2018 market bottoms.
Average drawdown post-entry exceeds 12%.

rationale: Evaluates if sudden bull-steepening in the 2s10s yield curve acts as a reliable exit signal for long-duration growth stocks.
expected_failure_mode: Bull steepening driven by rate-cut expectations might fuel a speculative bid in tech, defeating the exit.
stop_conditions: Strategy goes to cash and misses more than 20% of a subsequent tech rally.
Win rate of the exit signal is below 45% in confirmed recessions.

rationale: Tests whether rapid changes in the 10Y-2Y Treasury spread predict regime shifts before they manifest in tech price structure.
expected_failure_mode: The lag between yield-curve shifts and tech equity impacts varies drastically across cycles.
stop_conditions: Out-of-sample information coefficient falls below 0.05.
Fails to outperform standard moving-average filters on QQQ in the 2008-2010 window.

rationale: Tests if relative momentum divergence between semiconductors and software flags early institutional rotation in broad tech leadership.
expected_failure_mode: Sub-sector correlations converge during macro liquidations, wiping out granular lead time.
stop_conditions: Tracking error relative to TECL buy-and-hold during uptrends exceeds 15%.
Alpha contribution drops to zero across non-overlapping 3-year windows.

rationale: Tests whether cumulative volume-weighted price trends reveal hidden accumulation during extended flat consolidations.
expected_failure_mode: High-volume nodes frequently act as magnets rather than launching pads.
stop_conditions: Win rate drops below 45% over a 100-trade sample.
Maximum consecutive false signals exceeds 7.

rationale: Tests if deceleration in high-yield credit-spread widening marks reliable macro bottoms for leveraged tech assets.
expected_failure_mode: Spreads can plateau at elevated levels during prolonged recessions, causing premature entries.
stop_conditions: Drawdown during 2022 exceeds buy-and-hold drawdown by more than 5%.
Signal fails to trigger within 15 days of major historical troughs.

rationale: Tests if equal-weight tech indices outperforming market-cap variants marks broad-based sustainable rally participation.
expected_failure_mode: Mega-cap concentration can drive sustained rallies alone, causing missed secular advances when breadth is narrow.
stop_conditions: Underperforms TECL buy-and-hold by more than 20% during any secular bull-market year.
Correlation with existing breadth-thrust families exceeds 0.80.

rationale: Tests whether consecutive high-volume negative closes followed by low-volume rallies flag terminal trend decay.
expected_failure_mode: High-volume selling can represent brief index rebalancing rather than persistent institutional distribution.
stop_conditions: False positive exit rate exceeds 60% in secular bull markets.
Profit factor drops below 1.25 on a rolling 3-year basis.

rationale: Tests if localized volume spikes during steep drawdowns identify high-probability rebound entry windows.
expected_failure_mode: Volume climaxes can occur in stages during structural liquidations, repeatedly catching falling knives.
stop_conditions: Average drawdown within 5 days post-entry exceeds 12%.
Fails to outperform a cash benchmark during a major secular bear market.

rationale: Tests whether economic risk-on momentum from the copper/gold ratio provides long-lead filtering for leveraged tech exposure.
expected_failure_mode: Commodity dynamics can decouple from technology equity trends due to localized supply-chain shocks.
stop_conditions: Rolling 12-month correlation between copper/gold momentum and NDX returns falls below 0.3.
Total turnover exceeds 24 trades per year.

rationale: Tests whether decay in long-term momentum functions as an early warning exit before structural price breakdowns.
expected_failure_mode: Rallies frequently show multi-month momentum deceleration while price continues higher, leading to missed gains.
stop_conditions: The strategy exits more than 15% below the cyclical peak on 3 consecutive cycles.
Calmar ratio falls below buy-and-hold over the full era.

rationale: Tests if positive momentum in speculative corporate debt leads shifts in high-beta tech growth allocation.
expected_failure_mode: Central-bank interventions can decouple corporate debt markets from tech equity growth trajectories.
stop_conditions: Annualized alpha relative to QQQ drops below 2% over a 5-year rolling window.
Maximum strategy drawdown exceeds buy-and-hold during credit crunch periods.

rationale: Tests if waiting for the first weekly higher-low during a deep correction filters out false bear-market relief rallies.
expected_failure_mode: V-bottom recoveries can leave this family behind entirely due to late entries.
stop_conditions: Misses more than 35% of the initial rebound move on major bottoms.
Ulcer Index exceeds QQQ buy-and-hold over the full era.

rationale: Tests whether multi-day sequential inside bars represent institutional equilibrium before explosive trend-defining expansions.
expected_failure_mode: Inside-bar cascades often resolve into random low-liquidity whipsaws.
stop_conditions: Average win-to-loss ratio drops below 1.5:1.
Profitable signal percentage falls below 38% across a 200-sample run.

rationale: Tests whether a state machine tracking volume-weighted distribution-day velocity can map bull-to-bear regime transitions.
expected_failure_mode: Distribution days can cluster harmlessly during mid-cycle consolidations, producing false exits.
stop_conditions: Annualized turnover exceeds 40 trades per year.
Maximum missed drawup due to false exits exceeds 25% in a single year.

rationale: Tests whether the rate of change of the equity put/call ratio crossing its moving average signals a structural sentiment flip.
expected_failure_mode: Retail options volume dynamics can distort the ratio as a proxy for institutional capital intent.
stop_conditions: Win rate on long entries falls below 45% during choppy regimes.
Sharpe ratio on a 5-year rolling out-of-sample window drops below 0.4.

rationale: Tests if the percentage of Nasdaq-100 components outperforming the index identifies broad institutional accumulation.
expected_failure_mode: Index performance can stay concentrated in a few mega-cap names for years, causing missed advances.
stop_conditions: Underperforms QQQ by more than 15% over any rolling 24-month period.
Correlation with standard breadth metrics exceeds 0.85.

rationale: Tests if sudden DXY rate-of-change reversals lead global liquidity allocations into US mega-cap tech.
expected_failure_mode: Domestic growth factors or tech-specific themes can override global currency trends.
stop_conditions: Information coefficient between weekly DXY changes and NDX returns falls below 0.05.
Drawdown is 1.2x larger than buy-and-hold during a currency crisis.

rationale: Tests if rapid multi-month liquidations in margin debt reliably flag ultimate macro capitulation floors.
expected_failure_mode: Margin debt can keep unwinding or plateau at depressed levels for years after a structural bubble burst.
stop_conditions: The strategy enters more than 30% above the eventual cyclical bear-market bottom.
Out-of-sample Sharpe ratio drops below 0.35 over a 10-year cycle.

rationale: Tests if localized options open-interest concentrations function as institutional support during market drawdowns.
expected_failure_mode: Gamma walls can break or shift lower as market makers dynamically hedge.
stop_conditions: More than 25% of gamma-wall entries experience immediate breakthroughs exceeding 5%.
Underperforms a simple 200-day moving-average filter over the long era.

rationale: Tests if price distance from a rolling volatility-adjusted corridor captures deep structural extensions.
expected_failure_mode: During strong trends, price can ride the corridor edge indefinitely, causing severe under-exposure or premature exits.
stop_conditions: Underperforms buy-and-hold by more than 25% during a sustained 12-month bull market.
Maximum drawdown reduction versus buy-and-hold is less than 5%.
